    Home › Aircraft › Tupolev Tu-114

    Tupolev Tu-114

    Tupolev · Turboprop airliner · Retired

    The fastest propliner in history.

    Length54.1 m
    Wingspan51.1 m
    Range8,800 km
    MTOW175 t
    Typical seats170
    Built32
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#26 of 35
    3.14 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#38 of 65
    50.3 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#22 of 68
    0.94 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#28 of 68
    3.23 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#21 of 65
    10.1 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Weight per seat#5 of 35
    1029 kg MTOW ÷ typical seats
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Tupolev Tu-114's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Tupolev Tu-11451.1 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #28 of 68 by length → #30 by weight → #50 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length54.10 m
    Wingspan51.10 m
    Height15.50 m
    MTOW175,000 kg
    Cruise770 km/h — propeller record
    Range≈8,800 km
    Engines4 × Kuznetsov NK-12MV (contra-rotating)
    First flight15 November 1957
    Built32

    Compare it

    vs Antonov An-22 vs Ilyushin Il-62 Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousSukhoi Su-57 Felon Next →Tupolev Tu-144

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The Tu-114 was a Tu-95 strategic bomber with an airliner fuselage — the fastest way for the USSR to get intercontinental range in 1957. It kept the bomber's swept wings and four monstrous NK-12 turboprops with contra-rotating propellers, and cruised at 770 km/h: a propeller speed record that has never been broken.

    When Khrushchev flew it to New York in 1959, the aircraft was so tall no American airstairs could reach the door. It flew Moscow to Havana nonstop over 10,000 km, and in partnership with JAL carried the first Moscow–Tokyo services.

    Only 32 were built, retired by 1976 — but its bomber parent flies on, and every droning Tu-95 that NATO intercepts today is powered by the same engines that made the Tu-114 the fastest propliner ever.

    Common questions

    Tupolev Tu-114 — the short answers

    How fast is the Tupolev Tu-114?
    The Tupolev Tu-114 cruises at about 870 km/h — roughly 470 knots, or 541 mph.
    How far can the Tupolev Tu-114 fly?
    The Tupolev Tu-114 has a maximum range of about 8,800 km (4,752 nautical miles, 5,469 miles). At its cruise speed that is roughly 10.1 hours in the air.
    How many people does the Tupolev Tu-114 carry?
    A typical Tupolev Tu-114 carries 170 passengers. Exact figures vary with the operator's chosen configuration.
    Is the Tupolev Tu-114 still in production?
    Status: Retired. The Tupolev Tu-114 first flew in 1957, and about 32 have been built.
    How big is the Tupolev Tu-114?
    The Tupolev Tu-114 is 54.1 m long with a wingspan of 51.1 m and a height of 15.5 m. Its maximum takeoff weight is 175,000 kg.
    Variants

    The Tupolev Tu-114 line

    Tu-114
    The airliner, 1957–1976.
    Tu-126
    The radar-picket derivative.
    Operators

    Who flies the Tupolev Tu-114

    No current operators — this aircraft's flying days are over. The operators of record are below.

    RUAeroflotThe sole airline operator — flagship international routes, 1961–1976.—

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
